header_summary:
Recent discussions in the Bitcoin development community, led by contributors in various forums, have addressed innovative uses of blockchain technology to enhance AI and transaction security. One significant development involves implementing data-carrying annexes in P2TR transactions to supervise AI agents, ensuring their computations are accurate and verifiable. This application is particularly crucial in scenarios requiring high accountability, such as the hypothetical survival solutions on an isolated island, where AI agents must operate within strict energy constraints ([source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CALZpt+GGORG3bgM0C3sQYVNbc1W7aFyb0qP_c2xbZ8f64S_ksQ@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m49c2ecc7d787093a0fb97de67ccbedde0419eb51)).

Enhancements in wallet software are also being discussed, focusing on transaction replacements without the need for a signaling bit, which aids in managing blockchain transactions securely and user-friendly. This feature accommodates transactions from legacy wallets, ensuring users can replace transactions safely across multiple devices, thus preventing potential risks or fund loss ([source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/178162039498.7.16213177200751410070.1421232845@slmail.me/T/#mf1437c3fbbe517e3e2976bec1440f07c45312cc7)).

Privacy concerns in blockchain transactions have prompted the development of tools like [am-i.exposed](https://am-i.exposed), which help users assess the privacy levels of their transactions. A new system has been introduced to run privacy heuristics privately, using cryptographic techniques to ensure no sensitive wallet information is leaked to servers or third parties, enhancing user security against common vulnerabilities such as address reuse and poor coin control ([source](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/privately-running-wallet-privacy-heuristics-on-your-own-utxos-without-needing-to-run-your-own-node/2625)).

Lastly, the application of Fountain Codes is being explored to reduce the storage demands on Bitcoin nodes, allowing more efficient data management and scalability within the network. This technique enables any node to reconstruct the blockchain with only a portion of the total encoded data, significantly decreasing individual node storage requirements. More insights into this method and its integration with blockchain technology are detailed in a blog post by Vinteum ([source](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/fountain-codes-a-way-to-reduce-blockchain-storage-costs/2624)).

recent_posts.title: Accountable Computing On-Chain Contracts for AI Agents Supervision
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin P2TR transactions may soon support data-carrying annexes for AI supervision.
- Such annexes can ensure AI responses are accurate and computationally verifiable.
- Using Bitcoin's blockchain aligns incentives and minimizes asymmetry in AI interactions.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Removal of BIP 125 RBF signalling in wallet transactions
recent_posts.summary:
- The wallet software supports transaction replacements through RBF, even without a signaling bit.
- It includes warnings to inform users of risks in replacing transactions across multiple devices.
- The software is designed to balance ease of use with secure transaction management.

recent_posts.title: Privately running wallet privacy heuristics on your own UTXOs without needing to run your own node
recent_posts.summary:
- Recent tools assess privacy leaks in blockchain wallet transactions.
- A new system allows private heuristic checks without revealing wallet data.
- The system employs cryptographic measures to ensure transactional privacy.

recent_posts.title: Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
recent_posts.summary:
- Fountain Codes segment Bitcoin's blockchain into encoded segments for efficient reconstruction.
- Collaborative research with Vinteum aims to enhance Bitcoin scalability using Fountain Codes.
- Detailed findings and methodologies are explored in a comprehensive blog post.

recent_posts.title: K-of-N threshhold key generation scheme by limited shared secrets
recent_posts.summary: - Advanced cryptographic schemes enhance multisignature protocols using MuSig2 standards.  - Shared secrets improve Lightning network functionality through combinatorial signers' secrets.  - These enhancements fortify decentralized network security and streamline multisignature operations.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a new Bitcoin rule targeting specific 64-byte transaction encodings.
- It addresses SPV malleability by invalidating blocks with certain Merkle tree preimages.
- Implementation guidance includes a C++ function and miner procedures for block validation.

active_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts.summary:
- The modification to BIP360 mandates merkle paths in P2MR's control blocks.
- It aims to discourage simpler, less secure script practices in P2MR addresses.
- Feedback is sought, especially from P2TRv2 proponents, on the protocol's impact.

active_posts.title: Proposal discussion: BIP39 native-language display wordlists
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances Bitcoin wallet recovery with native language support for BIP39 phrases.
- It aims to improve user confidence and reduce errors without changing existing cryptographic inputs.
- Feedback is requested on the draft available at a specified GitHub repository for further development.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances BIP-360 by optimizing EC public key recovery in P2MR structures.
- It employs recoverable EC leaves, reducing witness size and matching the P2WPKH efficiency.
- The optimization aligns with pre-activation integration, optimizing blockchain space and transaction costs.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- A Bitcoin-native LLM aims to enhance script and protocol interactions.
- It requires a large dataset from diverse Bitcoin-centric sources for development.
- The model's architecture includes offline analysis and live data querying layers.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy efforts focus on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integration, not Layer 1.
- Cross-Input Signature Aggregation might lower costs but doesn't boost privacy itself.
- Regulatory compatibility and minimal protocol impact guide current Bitcoin privacy solutions.

today_in_history_posts.title: MAD-HTLC
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Recent findings highlight security dependencies in HTLCs reliant on miner altruism.
- An alternative, MAD-HTLC, offers enhanced, provably secure transaction systems.
- Additional insights and feedback mechanisms are detailed in linked resources.

today_in_history_posts.title: Dynamic Commitments: Upgrading Channels Without On-Chain Transactions
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rusty proposes adding `commit_switch` and `commit_switch_reply` to implement double-opt-in.
- He suggests a linear progression for channel types to simplify testing.
- Upgrades involve `commit_switch`, feature matching, and may result in no operation if incompatible.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Investment strategies include passive and active types with varying management levels.  - Passive investment issues like the principal/agent problem may lead to underutilized assets.  - The article discusses stabilizing economies using a mix of inflationary and deflationary currencies.
